step_by_step_thinking: I agree with agent1 that the maximum level of alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) is indeed seen in fetal serum for several reasons. First, hepatic cell development and function increase dramatically during fetal life, leading to higher AFP synthesis compared to postnatal periods. Second, while AFP does cross into maternal circulation via placental transport mechanisms, these amounts do not reach the same high levels observed within the fetus itself. Therefore, my final answer remains consistent with option A. Final
